label: Effectiveness of a Digital Health Intervention Leveraging Reinforcement Learning
authors: Aguilera A, Arevalo Avalos M, Xu J, Chakraborty B, Figueroa C, Garcia F, Rosales K, Hernandez-Ramos R, Karr C, Williams J, Ochoa-Frongia L, Sarkar U, Yom-Tov E, Lyles C
headline: RL-based notification personalization improved PA in diabetes/depression patients
detail: RCT using RL to personalize digital health notifications for Spanish-speaking adults with diabetes/depression. RL arm significantly improved physical activity and mental health outcomes compared to control. RL for multi-behavior intervention. Complements PEARL in clinical population. Published JMIR Oct 2024. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Machine Learning Methods to Personalize Persuasive Strategies in mHealth Interve
authors: Brons A, Wang S, Visser B, Krose B, Bakkes S, Veltkamp R
headline: [Mixed] RL most common ML approach for timing personalization in PA mHealth, but effect sizes modest
detail: Scoping review of ML methods for personalizing persuasive strategies in PA mHealth interventions. Found RL was the dominant approach for timing of messages. Provides taxonomy of persuasion strategies and ML personalization methods. Provides taxonomy of ML-for-PA personalization. Valuable for situating our config-driven framework in the landscape. (Found in missions: 01)

label: A scoping review of methodologies for applying artificial intelligence to physic
authors: An R, Shen J, Wang J, Yang Y
venue: Journal of Sport and Health Science
headline: [Mixed] RL, ML, and DL all applied to PA intervention design; RL most promising for adaptive personalization
detail: Comprehensive scoping review of AI methodologies for PA interventions. Found RL applied mostly for adaptive intervention delivery and personalization. Notes significant heterogeneity in methodology and outcome reporting. Published online 2023, in print 2024. High-impact sports science journal. (Found in missions: 01)

label: An Evaluation of the Effect of App-Based Exercise Prescription Using Reinforceme
authors: Doherty C, Lambe R, O'Grady B, O'Reilly-Morgan D, Smyth B, Lawlor A, Hurley N, Tragos E
headline: RL-based exercise prescription improved user satisfaction and exercise intensity vs fixed schedules
detail: Randomized crossover trial comparing RL-based exercise prescription against fixed schedules. RL personalized workout difficulty and type. Found significantly higher user satisfaction and self-reported exercise intensity in RL arm. One of few RL-for-exercise trials with positive results. Small sample but crossover design. Published Nov 2024. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Did we personalize? Assessing personalization by an online reinforcement learnin
authors: Ghosh S, Kim R, Chhabria P, Dwivedi R, Klasnja P, Liao P, Zhang K, Murphy S
headline: [Mixed] Resampling-based test reveals HeartSteps RL personalization may be artifact of algorithm stochasticity
detail: Introduces resampling methodology to assess whether an RL algorithm truly personalizes treatments. Applied to HeartSteps V2 data. Finds apparent personalization in certain states may be artifact of RL stochasticity rather than genuine learning. Directly relevant to HeartSteps V2. Top ML journal. Authors include Murphy group leaders. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Smartphone App Using Reinforcement Learning for Obesity: Single-Arm Feasibility
authors: Kurisu K, Yamamoto Y, Aoyama T, Yamauchi T, Yoshiuchi K
headline: [Negative] Single-arm RL app for obesity showed no significant PA improvement; feasibility established
detail: Single-arm feasibility study of smartphone app using RL for personalized behavioral recommendations targeting weight loss and PA. Small sample, no control group. PA outcomes not significantly improved from baseline. Habituation and small sample cited as limitations. Explicitly reports null finding for PA outcomes. Important negative result for RL-for-PA literature. Feb 2026. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Impact of Iterative Development and Beta-Testing on the Usability and Acceptabil
authors: Vandelanotte C, Maher C, Hodgetts D, Imam T, Rashid M, To QG, Trost S
venue: Journal of Physical Activity and Health
headline: [Mixed] MoveMentor JITAI with ML back-end shows acceptable usability but mixed engagement
detail: Usability/acceptability evaluation of MoveMentor, a JITAI digital assistant using ML/RL for PA message personalization. Beta-testing showed acceptable usability (SUS ~68) but variable contextual engagement. Companion to MoveMentor protocol. Valuable for real-world deployment challenges. Published Jun 2025. (Found in missions: 01)

label: MoveMentor - examining effectiveness of a machine learning and app-based digital
authors: Vandelanotte C, Trost S, Hodgetts D, Imam T, Rashid M, To QG, Maher C
headline: Protocol for RCT of ML-based JITAI digital assistant for PA (MoveMentor)
detail: Two-arm RCT (n=300) evaluating ML/RL-based PA message personalization vs static comparison. Primary outcome: daily step count change at 12 weeks. Important upcoming trial. Results expected 2026-2027. Published Trials Jul 2025. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Automated Personalized Goal Setting for Individual Exercise Behavior: Protocol f
authors: Caro JC, Nguyen PH, Lipman S
headline: Protocol for contextual bandit-based goal recommendation for exercise (Apptivate)
detail: Protocol for adaptive intervention trial using contextual bandits for personalized goal setting. Three arms: user choice, CB recommendations, automated plans. n=500, 40-day intervention. Directly uses contextual bandits for PA. Addresses user autonomy question. Results expected 2026. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Promoting physical activity in middle-aged and older adults through digital inte
authors: Catellani P, Piastra M
headline: DRL message framing matched to user profiles improves PA motivation in older adults
detail: Proposes deep RL framework for matching message framing (gain vs loss) to user psychological profiles in PA intervention for older adults. Simulation-based evaluation shows 15-20% engagement improvement over static assignment. Uses DRL not contextual bandits. Simulation-based. Targets older adults. Published 2025. (Found in missions: 01)

label: A Reinforcement Learning Approach to Enhance Physical Activity and Reduce Fall R
authors: Liu C, Thiamwong L, Nguyen T, Suarez JR, Park JH, Lou Q, Xie R
headline: [Mixed] RL-based wearable feedback system for PA and fall risk in older adults (preliminary mixed results)
detail: Conference abstract presenting RL using wearable sensors for real-time PA feedback in older adults. RL agent learned personalized activity suggestions. 60% showed positive step trends, 40% showed no change. Conference abstract. Full paper may be separate. Related to KANDI paper by same group. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Tailored Behavior-Change Messaging for Physical Activity: Integrating Contextual
authors: Song H, Hofer D, Islambouli R, Hawkins L, Bhattacharjee A, Hassanzadeh Z, Smeddinck J, Franklin M, Williams JJ
headline: Hybrid cMABxLLM approach balances personalization and token efficiency in 30-day PA trial
detail: Deployed hybrid cMAB+LLM system in 30-day PA intervention. Compared 5 delivery models: randomization, cMAB-only, LLM-only, LLM+history, cMABxLLM. cMABxLLM matched LLM acceptance with ~60% fewer tokens, improved delivery balance across 4 intervention types. First deployed cMABxLLM hybrid for PA. Highly relevant to LLM+RL next steps. v4 Mar 2026. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Enhancing Adaptive Behavioral Interventions with LLM Inference from Participant-
authors: Karine K, Marlin BM
headline: LLM-inferred state descriptions improve RL policy learning in PA intervention simulation by 15-25%
detail: Proposes using LLMs to infer richer state representations from participant natural language, expanding state space without reducing data efficiency. Shows LLM-inferred states significantly improve online policy learning for RL-based JITAI. Extension of StepCountJITAI framework. Accepted at MLHC 2025. Direct extension of StepCountJITAI. Highly relevant to sensor FM + LLM next steps. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Diffusion Policies with Offline and Inverse Reinforcement Learning for Promoting
authors: Liu C, Thiamwong L, Fu Y, Xie R
headline: KANDI: Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks + Diffusion Policies for offline IRL in PA promotion outperform SOTA
detail: Introduces KANDI for offline inverse RL applied to PA in older adults at fall risk. Learns reward functions from expert behavior. Uses diffusion policies in actor-critic. Evaluated on PEER study data. Outperforms SOTA on D4RL benchmark. Accepted ICMLA 2025. Offline RL + IRL for PA. Novel use of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks. Preprint. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Precision Physical Activity Prescription via Reinforcement Learning for Function
authors: Lin G, Miao R, Sacheck J, Zhang X
headline: Offline RL learns personalized optimal step distributions from All of Us data (n~5000)
detail: Offline RL algorithm for learning personalized optimal daily step distributions over time, using All of Us data. Continuous action space (step distribution function). Learned policy recommends ~20% more steps with more consistent patterns. Subgroup-specific recommendations by glucose, BMI, BP, age, sex. First RL-PA paper with continuous functional action space. Real All of Us data. Preprint May 2026. (Found in missions: 01, 05)

label: A novel physical activity recognition approach using deep ensemble optimized tra
authors: Ahmadian S, Rostami M, Farrahi V, Oussalah M
headline: RL-optimized transformer ensemble for PA recognition (94% accuracy)
detail: Uses RL to optimize ensemble of transformers for PA recognition from wearable data. Activity recognition focus, not intervention design. Demonstrates RL use in PA domain for model optimization. Activity recognition, not intervention. Tangential. Published Neural Networks 2024. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Personalized Type 1 Diabetes Management: Reinforcement Learning-Based Insulin Do
authors: Taku EM, Gupta V, Singhal A
headline: [Mixed] RL for insulin dosing uses PA as context variable; reduces hypoglycemic events 30% in simulation
detail: RL-based insulin dosing system using PA as context variable affecting glucose. PA promotion not the goal, but demonstrates PA-aware RL for health. Reduced hypoglycemic events ~30% vs static regimen in simulation. PA is context feature not intervention target. Tangential. Published JMIR Diabetes Jun 2026. (Found in missions: 01)

label: Artificial Intelligence-Enabled Mobile Health Intervention (mDiabetes) to Reduce
authors: Chadwick J, Jaswal N, Surya J, Sivamani C, Ramesan V, Saligram N
headline: AI-powered personalized messages improved PA and diet in rural India (n=1048 pre-post)
detail: Quasi-experimental pre-post study of AI-enabled mHealth for diabetes risk reduction in rural India. AI personalized message content. Improved PA and dietary outcomes vs static messages. Uses rule-based AI, not RL specifically. AI-personalized not RL-specific. Large sample. Pre-post design limits inference. Dec 2025. (Found in missions: 01)

label: A Scoping Review on Artificial Intelligence-Supported Interventions for Nonpharm
authors: Shah N, Castellanos A, Chen Y, Piette J, Bucher A, Murphy S
headline: [Mixed] AI-supported PA interventions for rheumatic diseases show promise but limited RL evidence
detail: Scoping review of AI-supported nonpharmacologic interventions (including PA) for chronic rheumatic diseases. Limited RL use; most AI used simpler ML methods. Identifies opportunity for RL in PA for arthritis patients. Reviews AI for PA in rheumatic disease. Limited direct RL relevance. Published Feb 2026. (Found in missions: 01)

label: The Micro-Randomized Trial for Developing Digital Interventions: Data Analysis M
authors: Tianchen Qian, Michael A. Russell, Linda M. Collins, Predrag Klasnja, Stephanie T. Lanza, Hyesun Yoo, Susan A. Murphy
headline: Introduced the WCLS estimator for causal excursion effects in MRTs with worked HeartSteps examples
detail: Provides formal definition of causal excursion effects for MRT data analysis and introduces the weighted and centered least-squares (WCLS) estimator. Covers primary and secondary analyses with SAS/R implementation. Uses HeartSteps MRT as running example throughout. Foundational MRT methods paper; defines causal excursion effects and WCLS estimator used across all subsequent MRT analyses. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Multi-Level Micro-Randomized Trial: Detecting the Proximal Effect of Messages on
authors: Jing Xu, Xiaoxi Yan, Caroline Figueroa, Joseph Jay Williams, Bibhas Chakraborty
headline: Extended MRT design to multi-level intervention components with GEE-based sample size calculators
detail: Proposes multi-level MRT (MLMRT) design allowing more than two levels per intervention component. Develops GEE-based test statistics for proximal effects and associated sample size calculators. Motivated by DIAMANTE smartphone study for physical activity in diabetes and mental health. R Shiny app provided. First paper to extend MRT framework beyond binary treatments; R Shiny app for sample size calculation. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Assessing Time-Varying Causal Effect Moderation in the Presence of Cluster-Level
authors: Jieru Shi, Zhenke Wu, Walter Dempsey
headline: Extended causal excursion effects to cluster-level heterogeneity and interference in MRTs
detail: Revisits causal excursion effects under potential cluster-level treatment effect heterogeneity and interference. Tests moderation by cluster-level variables using data from multi-institution Intern Health Study MRT of first-year medical residents. First MRT paper to relax between-subject independence and non-interference assumptions. (Found in missions: 02)

label: The Micro-Randomized Trial for Developing Digital Interventions: Experimental De
authors: Tianchen Qian, Ashley E. Walton, Linda M. Collins, Predrag Klasnja, Stephanie T. Lanza, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Mashifiqui Rabbi, Michael A. Russell, Maureen A. Walton, Hyesun Yoo, Susan A. Murphy
headline: Comprehensive tutorial on why, when, and how to use MRTs for JITAI development
detail: Comprehensive review covering MRT planning, design, implementation, primary and secondary analyses. Covers causal excursion effects, WCLS estimator, and software implementation. Uses HeartSteps, SARA, and BariFit MRTs as illustrations. Clarifies design considerations for researchers new to MRTs. Definitive tutorial for practitioners; overlaps with arXiv:2004.10241 and arXiv:2005.05880. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Estimating Time-Varying Direct and Indirect Causal Excursion Effects with Longit
authors: Jieru Shi, Zhenke Wu, Walter Dempsey
headline: Extended MRT causal framework to decompose direct and indirect effects through time-varying mediators
detail: Develops methodology for decomposing total causal excursion effects into direct and indirect effects in MRTs with binary outcomes. Addresses time-varying mediation in mobile health intervention settings where intermediate variables may be affected by treatment. Bridges causal mediation analysis with the MRT causal excursion effect framework. (Found in missions: 02)

label: A Meta-Learning Method for Estimation of Causal Excursion Effects to Assess Time
authors: Jieru Shi, Walter Dempsey
headline: Proposed doubly robust DR-WCLS estimator handling unknown randomization, missing data, and high-dimensional features
detail: Proposes DR-WCLS, a flexible doubly robust inferential procedure for causal excursion effects from a meta-learner perspective. Handles uncertain randomization probabilities, missing observations, and high-dimensional mHealth data. Demonstrates consistent and more efficient estimates than existing WCLS methods. Key advance: DR-WCLS relaxes three major WCLS assumptions simultaneously. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Incorporating Auxiliary Variables to Improve the Efficiency of Time-Varying Trea
authors: Jieru Shi, Zhenke Wu, Walter Dempsey
headline: Showed auxiliary variable adjustment improves MRT estimation efficiency with local efficiency guarantees
detail: Proposes method to incorporate auxiliary variables beyond prespecified moderators into MRT estimation to reduce chance imbalances and improve asymptotic efficiency. Extends to post-treatment auxiliary variables for lagged treatment effects. Demonstrated on Intern Health Study data. Addresses practical issue of underutilized sensor data in MRT analyses. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Estimating Causal Effects for Binary Outcomes Using Per-Decision Inverse Probabi
authors: Yihan Bao, Lauren Bell, Elizabeth Williamson, Claire Garnett, Tianchen Qian
headline: Reduced IPW variance in MRT binary outcomes by 2x+ using per-decision IPW
detail: Proposes per-decision IPW estimators that avoid variance inflation from product weighting when later treatments occur within the outcome window. Second estimator adds semiparametric efficiency projection. Demonstrated substantial efficiency improvement over standard IPW. Key practical advance for binary proximal outcomes common in mHealth (e.g., app opens). (Found in missions: 02)

label: Incorporating nonparametric methods for estimating causal excursion effects in m
authors: Xueqing Liu, Tianchen Qian, Lauren Bell, Bibhas Chakraborty
headline: Extended causal excursion effects to zero-inflated count outcomes with nonparametric estimation
detail: Develops estimation approaches for causal excursion effects with zero-inflated count proximal outcomes. Incorporates nonparametric techniques with bidirectional asymptotics. Motivated by and applied to Drink Less MRT for alcohol reduction. Addresses a common but previously unaddressed outcome type in MRTs: zero-inflated counts. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Efficient and Globally Robust Causal Excursion Effect Estimation
authors: Zhaoxi Cheng, Lauren Bell, Tianchen Qian
headline: Derived semiparametric efficiency bound for CEE and proposed efficient globally robust estimators
detail: Derives semiparametric efficiency bound for causal excursion effects with identity/log links. Proposes two-stage estimators achieving the efficiency bound and robust to misspecified nuisance models. Establishes general theory for globally robust Z-estimators. Demonstrated substantial efficiency gains on Drink Less MRT data. Theoretical ceiling: derives the fundamental efficiency bound for CEE estimation. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Data integration methods for micro-randomized trials
authors: Easton Huch, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Lindsey Potter, Cho Lam, David W. Wetter, Walter Dempsey
headline: Developed meta-analytic data integration combining multiple MRTs for efficiency gains
detail: Develops methods to integrate data from multiple related MRTs to improve statistical efficiency. Uses multivariate precision-weighted meta-estimator with asymptotic optimality. Applied to two smoking cessation MRTs. Published in Biometrics 2025. First paper to formally address MRT data integration. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Modeling Time-Varying Effects of Mobile Health Interventions Using Longitudinal
authors: Jiaxin Yu, Tianchen Qian
headline: First semiparametric varying-coefficient model for time-varying MRT effects using functional outcomes
detail: Proposes first semiparametric causal excursion effect model with varying coefficients for time-varying effects within and across decision points. Uses double time indices for longitudinal functional step count outcomes. HeartSteps analysis shows prompt effects vary by context (sedentary, weekday, suggestion type). Directly relevant to project context: HeartSteps analysis with granular step count outcomes. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Doubly Robust Estimation of Causal Excursion Effects in Micro-Randomized Trials
authors: Jiaxin Yu, Tianchen Qian
headline: First doubly robust CEE estimator handling MAR missing data in MRTs
detail: Two-stage doubly robust estimator for causal excursion effects when longitudinal outcomes are missing at random. Handles continuous, binary, and count outcomes. Consistent and asymptotically normal if either missingness or outcome model is correct. Demonstrated on HeartSteps data. Addresses missing data problem pervasive in mHealth MRTs (sensor non-wear, missed self-reports). (Found in missions: 02)

label: Evaluation of the HeartSteps Online Sampling Algorithm
authors: Xiang Meng, Walter Dempsey, Peng Liao, Nick Reid, Pedja Klasnja, Susan Murphy
headline: HeartSteps V2V3 online randomization algorithm successfully met intervention frequency and uniformity constraints
detail: Case study evaluating the online sampling algorithm used in HeartSteps V2V3 MRT to manage two constraints: average 1.5 interventions/day and uniform delivery across decision times. Algorithm performed well on both metrics. Provides practical recommendations for constrained MRT randomization. Directly relevant to HeartSteps constraint handling; important for power-constrained exploration in MRTs. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Distal Causal Excursion Effects: Modeling Long-Term Effects of Time-Varying Trea
authors: Tianchen Qian
headline: Introduced distal causal excursion effects (DCEE) for long-term behavior change in MRTs
detail: Novel estimand DCEE quantifies long-term effects of time-varying treatments on distal outcomes. Contrasts outcomes under two excursion policies while marginalizing over most treatment assignments. HeartSteps analysis: prompts delivered earlier in the study had stronger long-term habit formation effects. Critical for project: bridges MRT proximal effects to long-term behavior change outcomes. (Found in missions: 02)
